title: Masked Self-Attention
concept: During decoding, we mask future tokens so the model can only look at preceding words, preventing it from 'cheating' during training.

exercises.prompt: Why do we need to mask tokens during AI training?

options.text: To prevent the model from seeing future words and cheating
To make the words easier to read for humans

exercises.explain: In text generation, we want the AI to predict the next word. If we don't hide the future words, it's like giving a student the answer sheet during an exam!

cards.title: No Cheating Allowed!
cards.body: When an AI generates text, how does it learn to predict the next word without cheating? If it could see the whole sentence during training, it would just copy the answers! Let's discover how AI wears 'blinders' to learn properly.

cards.title: What is Masked Self-Attention?
cards.body: Masked self-attention is a special tool used in AI generators. It hides (masks) all the words that come after the current word. This ensures that when the AI is learning to write a sentence, it only looks at what has already been written, never at the future words.

cards.title: The Mystery Novel Shield
cards.body: Imagine reading a mystery novel, but you want to guess the ending fairly. To prevent yourself from cheating, you place a heavy cardboard card over the upcoming pages, only sliding it down one line at a time. Masked self-attention acts just like that cardboard card—it blocks the AI from looking ahead at the 'future' words it is supposed to guess next.

cards.title: Predicting the Next Word
cards.body: If the AI is trying to complete the sentence "The cat sat on the...", we want it to predict "mat". During training, the masked attention mechanism blocks the word "mat" (and any words after it) from the AI's view. It can only use "The", "cat", "sat", "on", and "the" to make its guess!

cards.title: Not a Privacy Filter!
cards.body: People often think that masking is used to hide private user data or bad words. Actually, it has nothing to do with privacy! It is a mathematical guardrail that prevents the AI from looking ahead in time while it learns to generate text.

cards.title: Masked vs. Unmasked Attention
cards.body: See how masking changes what the AI is allowed to look at when processing the word 'sat' in a sentence.

left.text: The AI sees: 'The cat sat' and hides 'on the mat' so it must guess what comes next.

right.text: The AI sees: 'The cat sat on the mat'. It knows the answer instantly without trying.

exercises.prompt: What would happen if we did NOT use masked self-attention when training a text generator?

options.text: The AI would write much faster
The AI would cheat by looking ahead and fail to learn properly
The AI would forget all the words it ever knew
The text would automatically be translated to French

exercises.explain: Without masking, the AI would see the entire sentence including the words it is supposed to predict. This would make training useless because the AI would just copy the answers instead of learning to generate them.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Masked self-attention blocks the AI from seeing both past words and future words.
exercises.explain: Masked self-attention blocks future words (to the right of the current word), but it allows the AI to fully look at all the past words (to the left) for context.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete how masked self-attention helps the AI learn.
exercises.sentence: Masked self-attention blocks the AI from looking at the _______ tokens during training.
exercises.explain: By hiding future words, the AI is forced to learn how to predict the next word based only on the past words it has already seen.

exercises.prompt: Which of these models is cheating by looking at the future words (not using a mask) while trying to predict the word after 'sat'?
exercises.explain: The AI is cheating! Sentence B has access to future words ("on the mat") which is exactly what it is trying to predict next. Sentence A shows proper masking where future words are blocked out.

exercises.prompt: Why is a mask applied to the attention matrix during the training of a text generator?

options.text: To make the model run faster on smaller screens
To stop it from looking at future words it needs to predict
To erase punctuation marks from the sentences
To translate the text into multiple languages instantly

exercises.explain: Masked self-attention is essential during training because it simulates real-world generation. In real life, the model has to predict the next word without knowing what lies ahead, so we must hide future tokens during training too.
